para_trans: Forest Land- Sindhudurg
Diversion of 18.94 ha. forest land for
construction of Minor Irrigation Tank
in Village Nirukhe, Taluka-Kudal,
Dist. Sindhudurg in the State of
Maharashtra.
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Order No.: FLD-2022/CR-285/F-10
Mantralaya, Mumbai-400 032.
Ref.:
1) Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur, Letter No. Desk-17/Nodal/Kolhapur/ID-13121/(29)/1603/2022-23, dt.27.09.2022.
2) Revenue and Forest Department, Government of Maharashtra, Government letter No. FLD- 2022/C.R.285/F-10, dt.20.03.2023.
3) Regional Office, Munistry of Environment, Forests & Climate Change, Government of India, Nagpur Letter No. FC-II/MH-211/2023-NGP/12885, dt.
4) Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur, Letter No. Desk-17/FCA-S2/PID-127453/Kolhapur/25-26/783, dt.02.07.2025.
5) Revenue and Forest Department, Government of Maharashtra, Government letter No. FLD- 2022/C.R.285/F-10, dt.08.07.2025.
6) Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate Change, Government of India, Nagpur Letter No.FC-II/MH-211/2023-NGP/14484, dt.
7) Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur, Letter No. Desk-17/FCA-S2/PID-127453/Kolhapur/25-26/2458, dt.14.01.2026.
Order :
Whereas, the Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer,
Maharashtra State, Nagpur, vide his letter under   reference No.1 has submitted a proposal for
diversion of 18.94 ha. forest land for construction of Minor Irrigation Tank in Village
Nirukhe, Taluka-Kudal, Dist. Sindhudurg in the State of Maharashtra;
AND whereas, the State Government vide under letter reference No.2 has sent the
proposal for approval of Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate
Change, Government of India, Nagpur;
AND whereas, the Government of India after due consideration, vide letter under
Reference No.3, accorded 'In-principle' approval to divert the 18.94 ha. Reserved and Identified
Forest land for the above project under section 2 Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan)
Adhiniyam, 1980. The said approval is subject to fulfillment of certain conditions by the User
Agency;
Government Order No.: FLD-2022/CR-285/F-10
AND whereas, the Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal
Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur, vide letter under reference No.4 has submitted
compliance of those conditions to the Government of Maharashtra;
AND whereas, the State Government vide under   letter reference No.5 has sent the
proposal for approval of the Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate
Change, Government of India, Nagpur;
AND whereas, the Regional Office, Ministry of Environment, Forests & Climate
Change, Government of India, Nagpur, wide under reference no.6 Accorded 'Final approval'
to divert the 18.94 ha. of forest land in favour of The Executive Engineer, Minor Irrigation
Division, Oros-Sindudurg for construction of Minor Irrigation Tank. Village Nirukhe,
Taluka-Kudal, Dist. Sindhudurg in the State of Maharashtra;
AND whereas, the Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal
Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur vide letter under reference No.7 has requested State
Government to issue diversion order in the matter;
NOW, therefore, Government of Maharashtra hereby directs that the area of 18.94 ha.
consisting of Reserved & Identified Forest Land more specifically described in the proposal
submitted vide letter under reference No. | and as per details given in the Schedule given below, be diverted to the User Agency, 1.e. The Executive Engineer, Minor Irrigation
Division, Oros-Sindhudurg for construction of Minor Irrigation Tank in Village Nirukhe,
Taluka-Kudal, Dist. Sindhudurg 1n the State of Maharashtra;
The diversion shall be effected subject to the terms and conditions mentioned in the
letter dated 15.02.2024 and dated 18.07.2025 of Regional Office, MoEF&CC, Nagpur. This
approval for diversion of forest land is also subject to compliance of following conditions by
the User Agency-
The User Agency shall comply with all the conditions stipulated in the 'in-principle' and
'Final approval 'letters of Regional Office, MoEF & CC, Nagpur, referred above. The
Chief Conservator of Forests (T), Kolhapur shall ensure the strict compliance of the
conditions by the User Agency and shall furnish the report in this regard to Additional
Principal Chief Conservator of Forests and Nodal Officer, Maharashtra State, Nagpur
from time to time.
2. Non-compliance of any of the conditions mentioned in Regional Office, MoEF&CC letters dated 15.02.2024 and dated 18.07.2025, in this order and Rules & Guidelines
made / issued under Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980, shall
amount to violation of Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980 and
action would be taken accordingly.
3. In case of non-compliance of any of the above conditions, the Chief Conservator of Forests (T), Kolhapur shall report through the State Government to the Deputy
Director General of Forests (Central), Regional Office, Ministry of Environment,
Forests & Climate Change, Government of India, Nagpur as per procedure laid down in guidelines under the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980.

Collector- Sindhudurg, Chief Executive Officer, Zilla Parishad, Sindhudurg and
Deputy Conservator of Forests, Sawantwadi are hereby directed to forward the
copy of this order to concerned Heads of Local Bodies, Panchayats, Municipal Bodies in addition to the relevant offices of the Government and they shall also
ensure the display of this order on their notice board as well as on the notice
board of local self-government bodies for the information of general public.
I. The Executive Engineer, Minor Irrigation Division, Oros-Sindhudurg, 15 hereby
directed that they should publish the entire order of forest clearances granted in
verbatim along with the details of Survey No., Gut No., area and legal status of
each Survey, Gut No. etc., along with the conditions and safe guards imposed by
the State Government while granting forest clearances in two widely circulated
daily newspapers, one in vernacular language and other in English language.
Copy of the Newspapers be submitted to Chief Conservator of Forests
(Territorial), Kolhapur, Deputy Conservator of Forests, Sawantwad1.
Heads of Local Bodies, Panchayats and Municipal Bodies in addition to the
relevant offices of the Government are hereby directed to display the same on
their notice board for 30 days from the date of receipt for the information of
general public.
